rationale: Both papers explicitly study business-cycle effects (citing note: unemployment-driven changes in drinking behavior; cited note: expansion/contraction effects on price and advertising elasticities), so the citation could be conceptually relevant rather than purely incidental. However, the matched evidence is only a DOI-level reference with no in-text context or stated methodological dependence, so there is not enough evidence to confidently classify it as clearly substantive or clearly mention-only.
